# Credit Default Swaps L2

> Analyse credit default swaps — single-name and index CDS, mechanics, valuation (CDS spread vs credit curve), upfront payment, basis trades, and CDS uses in credit risk management

**Category:** Trading · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fixed-income · cds · credit-derivatives · basis-trade · cdx · itraxx · cfa-level-2

## What this does

Analyse credit default swaps — single-name and index CDS, mechanics, valuation (CDS spread vs credit curve), upfront payment, basis trades, and CDS uses in credit risk management

Specifies 2 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **trade_id** *(text, required)* — CDS trade identifier
- **cds_type** *(select, required)* — single_name | index | basket | tranche

## What must be true

- **cds_basics → contract:** Protection buyer pays periodic premium to seller; seller compensates loss on credit event
- **cds_basics → notional:** Reference amount; not exchanged at inception
- **cds_basics → coupon:** Standardised at 100 bps (IG) or 500 bps (HY)
- **cds_basics → upfront_payment:** Reconciles standard coupon with market spread
- **credit_events_isda → bankruptcy:** Issuer files or fails to pay creditors
- **credit_events_isda → failure_to_pay:** Past grace period
- **credit_events_isda → restructuring:** Modified definitions for sovereign vs corporate
- **credit_events_isda → determinations_committee:** ISDA decides if event triggered
- **settlement → physical:** Buyer delivers bond, receives par
- **settlement → cash:** Auction determines recovery; cash payment of (1-recovery) × notional
- **settlement → auction_protocol:** Standard since 2009
- **single_name_vs_index → single_name:** One reference entity
- **single_name_vs_index → index:** CDX (NA), iTraxx (Europe); equally weighted basket
- **single_name_vs_index → sub_indices:** IG, HY, EM, financials
- **cds_spread → intuition:** Annualised premium that equates expected protection payments to expected losses
- **cds_spread → relationship:** CDS spread ≈ PD × LGD (approx; ignoring funding/liquidity)
- **cds_pricing → upfront_formula:** Upfront ≈ (CDS spread − fixed coupon) × duration × notional
- **cds_pricing → pv_protection_leg:** Σ PD_t × LGD × DF_t
- **cds_pricing → pv_premium_leg:** Σ coupon × DF_t × (1 − cumulative PD_t)
- **credit_curve → upward_sloping:** Higher cumulative PD over longer horizons
- **credit_curve → inverted_at_distress:** Near-term default risk dominant
- **uses_of_cds → hedging:** Buy protection on bond holdings
- **uses_of_cds → speculation:** Naked long/short credit exposure
- **uses_of_cds → arbitrage:** Basis trade between CDS spread and bond Z-spread
- **uses_of_cds → capital_relief:** Synthetic securitisation
- **basis_trade → definition:** CDS basis = CDS spread − bond Z-spread
- **basis_trade → positive_basis:** Buy bond, buy protection (rare; funding stress)
- **basis_trade → negative_basis:** Sell bond/short, sell protection
- **basis_trade → drivers:** Funding, repo specials, contract specifics, deliverable squeeze
- **index_tranches → structure:** Equity (0-3%), mezzanine (3-7%), senior tranches
- **index_tranches → correlation_sensitivity:** Equity tranche short correlation; senior long correlation
- **validation → trade_required:** trade_id present
- **validation → valid_type:** cds_type in [single_name, index, basket, tranche]

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Analyse Cds** — when trade_id exists; cds_type in ["single_name","index","basket","tranche"], then call service; emit cds.analysed. _Why: Analyse CDS trade._

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Invalid Type** — when cds_type not_in ["single_name","index","basket","tranche"], then emit cds.rejected. _Why: Unsupported CDS type._ *(error: `CDS_INVALID_TYPE`)*

## Errors it can return

- `CDS_INVALID_TYPE` — cds_type must be one of the supported types

## Events

**`cds.analysed`**
  Payload: `trade_id`, `cds_type`, `spread_bps`, `upfront`, `mark_to_market`, `basis`

**`cds.rejected`**
  Payload: `trade_id`, `reason_code`

## Connects to

- **credit-analysis-models-l2** *(required)*
